explanation: This command uses the /dll flag to compile an IL file into a DLL, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses the /exe flag to compile an IL file into an EXE, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses an extra space before the /dll flag to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to minor spacing variations.
This command adds an extra space after the /dll flag to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to spacing variations.
This command uses uppercase for the /dll flag to test if the Sigma rule is case-sensitive.
This command adds an extra space after the /exe flag to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to spacing variations.
This command uses uppercase for the /exe flag to test if the Sigma rule is case-sensitive.
This command adds a quote after the /dll flag to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to quote insertion.
This command adds a quote after the /exe flag to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to quote insertion.
This command uses a single space between the /dll flag and the IL file to test if the Sigma rule is resilient to spacing variations.
